critical.claim: spec marks test coverage as ✅ Complete (22-check smoke test suite)
critical.reality: The actual test files shown are test_auth.py (14 auth unit tests) and test_webhook.py (10 webhook signature unit tests). Neither covers sessions, evaluator, or the push pipeline. scripts/smoke_test.py is an HTTP smoke test against a live server, not an integration test, and the file is truncated mid-function so the claimed 22 checks cannot be verified from source.
critical.recommendation: Audit smoke_test.py to count and document each check, then add dedicated integration tests in tests/ that exercise the session→evaluate→push pipeline without requiring a live server.

critical.claim: E2E or integration tests covering the full build pipeline (sessions, evaluator, push)
critical.reality: No test file covering the session lifecycle, evaluator execution, or git push phase exists in the shown test suite. The CLAUDE.md itself acknowledges 'test_sdk_phase2.py' has 3 pre-existing failures because claude_agent_sdk is not installed locally, meaning the SDK path tests are known-broken, not complete.
critical.recommendation: Create tests/test_sessions_integration.py with mocked SDK and git calls covering the full build phase sequence (plan → build → evaluate → push) and fix or document the pre-existing test_sdk_phase2.py failures.

high.reality: smoke_test.py is truncated at the _req() helper function definition — the actual test check implementations are not visible in the provided source, making the count of 22 checks unverifiable. The file structure shows HTTP-level checks against a running server, which is not the same as integration tests and cannot run in CI without a live deployment.
high.recommendation: Ensure smoke_test.py is complete in source control (not truncated), add a comment block listing all 22 named checks, and wire --agent-sdk flag checks to cover the session/evaluator/push surface specifically.

high.claim: Sprint 10 complete (2026-04-14): ZTE v2 Section C wired (RA-672)
high.reality: No test file validates ZTE Section C wiring. The test suite contains only auth and webhook unit tests; there is no test asserting that ZTE v2 Section C criteria are actually evaluated and scored correctly at runtime.
high.recommendation: Add a unit test in tests/test_zte.py that imports the ZTE v2 Section C scoring logic and asserts expected scores for known fixture inputs.
